name: Stickynote Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Stickynote Workflow. This workflow integrates 8 different services: filter, stickyNote, code, linearTrigger, set. It contains 11 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## 👋 How to use this template
This template shows how you can take any event from any service, transform its data and send an alert to your desired app. Here's how to use it:

1. Double click the `Slack` node and connect to your Slack account by creating a Credential.
2. Change the channel name in the `Slack` node to a channel or user you have in Slack.
2. Click the `Execute Workflow` button, then double click the nodes to see their input and output data

### To customize this template to you needs:
1. Enable or swap the `Linear trigger` with any service that fits your use case.
2. Change the data transformation to fit your needs
3. Adjust the Slack node or swap it with any node that fits your use case
4. Disable or remove the `When clicking "Execute Workflow"` and `Code` node

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### 1. Trigger step listens for new events































We added a `Linear trigger` that starts the workflow every time we have an `Issue` event int the `Product & Design` team. 

**You can replace this node with any trigger you wish, like [Jira]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Clickup]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [HubSpot]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Google Sheets]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} etc.**

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 3. Notify the right channel
















Last but not least we're sending a message to the `#important-bugs` channel in Slack.

**You can replace this node with any service like [Teams]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Telegram]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Email]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} etc.**

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
### 2. Filter and transform your data















We only want to notify the team, if the event is fired on creating an urgent bug.

To edit the nodes, simply drag and drop input data into the fields or change the values directly. **Besides filters, n8n does have other powerful transformation nodes like [Set]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [ItemList]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Code]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and many more.**

parameters.content:
## 👋 How to use this template
This template shows how you can take any event from any service, transform its data and send an alert to your desired app. Here's how to use it:

1. Double click the `Slack` node and connect to your Slack account by creating a Credential.
2. Change the channel name in the `Slack` node to a channel or user you have in Slack.
2. Click the `Execute Workflow` button, then double click the nodes to see their input and output data

### To customize this template to you needs:
1. Enable or swap the `Linear trigger` with any service that fits your use case.
2. Change the data transformation to fit your needs
3. Adjust the Slack node or swap it with any node that fits your use case
4. Disable or remove the `When clicking "Execute Workflow"` and `Code` node

### 1. Trigger step listens for new events































We added a `Linear trigger` that starts the workflow every time we have an `Issue` event int the `Product & Design` team. 

**You can replace this node with any trigger you wish, like [Jira]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Clickup]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [HubSpot]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Google Sheets]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} etc.**

### 3. Notify the right channel
















Last but not least we're sending a message to the `#important-bugs` channel in Slack.

**You can replace this node with any service like [Teams]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Telegram]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Email]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} etc.**

### 2. Filter and transform your data















We only want to notify the team, if the event is fired on creating an urgent bug.

To edit the nodes, simply drag and drop input data into the fields or change the values directly. **Besides filters, n8n does have other powerful transformation nodes like [Set]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [ItemList]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} [Code]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and many more.**

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Linear Trigger".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Execute Workflow"".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
